content: The ReAct pattern alternates Thought-Action-Observation steps, grounding agent decisions in real-time feedback. It remains the cornerstone of modern agent design in 2026, solving the limitation of LLMs that cannot interact with the real world while maintaining coherent strategy.

Plan-and-Execute separates strategic planning from tactical execution: a Planner creates a strategy, smaller models execute subtasks independently, a Re-planner adjusts if needed. Benchmarks show 92% task completion rate with 3.6x speedup over sequential ReAct.

Circuit breaker pattern uses three states: CLOSED (requests flow), OPEN (failures exceeded threshold, block requests), HALF-OPEN (after cooldown, allow one test request). Default: open after 5 consecutive failures, 30-60 second recovery timeout.

Exponential backoff with jitter: start at 1s, double each retry (2s, 4s, 8s), cap at 30s, add random jitter to prevent thundering-herd. Formula: delay = min(cap, base * 2^attempt) + random(0, base). Essential for multi-agent systems hitting same API.

Context engineering has replaced prompt engineering as the critical discipline for AI agents in 2026. Agent failures are primarily context failures, not model failures. Using 40% of context window gives better results than 90%.

MCP (Model Context Protocol) uses JSON-RPC 2.0 between hosts, clients, and servers. Three primitives: Tools (executable functions), Resources (read-only data), Prompts (templates). Streamable HTTP is the recommended transport. 28% of Fortune 500 implemented MCP by 2026.

A2A (Agent-to-Agent) protocol by Google enables inter-agent collaboration. Agents publish Agent Cards (JSON) describing capabilities. Supports task negotiation (accept/reject/counter), multi-turn dialogue, and auto-discovery. v0.3 under Linux Foundation with 150+ orgs.

MCP handles agent-to-tool communication. A2A handles agent-to-agent communication. Most production systems in 2026 use both: agents discover peers via A2A, invoke tools via MCP. This is the emerging industry standard pattern.

Keep fewer than 20 tools available per agent turn for best accuracy. Use tool search/deferred loading when agents need 30+ tools. Tool descriptions with example inputs/outputs produce better results than short descriptions.

Structured outputs with strict JSON Schema (additionalProperties:false, all fields required) guarantee model responses match schemas exactly. This eliminates parsing errors and retry loops. Use null union types for optional fields.

For 50+ agents across multiple domains, hierarchical orchestration is the only viable option. A top-level manager delegates to mid-level supervisors, who delegate to leaf workers. IBM research confirms this for large enterprise agent systems.

Agent handoff pattern (OpenAI Swarm): agents transfer control mid-conversation with full context. The receiving agent gets the conversation history and continues seamlessly. Simpler than ROOT's current delegation which creates separate task contexts.

Multi-agent consensus via council debates: agents independently analyze, present arguments, and vote. The diversity prediction theorem proves keeping diverse agents reduces collective error even if individual agents are mediocre.

Extremized aggregation corrects crowd under-confidence: p_agg = p_mean^alpha / (p_mean^alpha + (1-p_mean)^alpha) where alpha > 1. Good Judgment Project found alpha ≈ 2.5 optimal. Apply to ROOT's MiRo council predictions.

Mem0-style universal memory: automatic fact extraction from conversations, user-level vs agent-level memory separation, conflict resolution when memories contradict. ROOT's 3-layer memory could adopt Mem0's consolidation patterns.

Memory consolidation follows neuroscience's Complementary Learning Systems: hippocampus (short-term, episodic) + neocortex (long-term, statistical). Interleaved replay from short to long-term prevents catastrophic interference.

Prioritized experience replay: priority = |prediction_error| + epsilon. Memories with high surprise value (large errors) are replayed more often. During ROOT's memory decay cycle, prioritize strengthening high-surprise memories over uniform decay.

Agentic RAG embeds agents in retrieval: Self-reflective RAG evaluates own retrievals and re-queries when evidence is weak. A-RAG framework exposes hierarchical retrieval with keyword_search, semantic_search, and chunk_read tools.

Semantic chunking at 256-512 tokens is optimal for most retrieval tasks. Agentic chunking has an AI dynamically decide splits based on structure. Late chunking embeds entire documents first, then chunks only retrieved ones at query time.

30% of autonomous agent runs hit exceptions needing recovery: hallucinations, context overflow, API rate limits. Classify failures: transient (429, network glitch → retry) vs persistent (auth failure, provider outage → failover). Never retry persistent failures.

Agent fallback chain: Tier 1 (Opus for complex) → Tier 2 (Sonnet for standard) → Tier 3 (Haiku for simple) → Tier 4 (offline/local). Each tier gets independent circuit breakers. Log which tier served each request to track degradation.

Instrument agents with structured logs, metrics, and traces. Capture retry counts, fallback paths, failure reasons. Monitor p95 latency histograms. Log every decision point: routing choice, model selection, tool call, outcome. This feeds learning engines.

Agent tool use guardrails: validate all parameters before execution, set timeout per tool call (30s default), limit output size (truncate at 10KB), sandbox code execution (E2B/Docker), never execute tools without human approval for irreversible actions.

MetaGPT's shared message pool: all agents publish to a central pool, subscribe by role. This replaces direct agent-to-agent calls and enables emergent collaboration. ROOT's message bus with topic wildcards supports this pattern.

AutoGen's teachable agent concept: agents learn from user feedback within a session, storing corrections as retrievable memories. Combined with ROOT's learning engine, agents could self-correct based on outcome feedback.

Thompson Sampling for agent routing: maintain Beta(alpha=successes+1, beta=failures+1) posterior per agent. Sample from each, route to max sample. Provably optimal exploration-exploitation with regret O(sqrt(KT*ln(T))).

Maximum entropy routing adds entropy bonus to prevent premature convergence: pi* = argmax[sum(r(s,a)) + alpha*H(pi)]. Temperature alpha anneals from high (explore all agents) to low (exploit best). Based on Soft Actor-Critic.

Active inference unifies perception, action, and learning: minimize free energy F = D_KL[q(s)||p(s|o)] - ln p(o). Pragmatic value (achieving goals) + epistemic value (reducing uncertainty) naturally balanced from single objective.

Counterfactual regret learning: after each task, compute outcomes for alternative routing. Regret(a) = max_a' Q(s,a') - Q(s,a). Learn from what could have happened, not just what did. Much richer learning signal than outcome-only feedback.

Small-world network topology for 162+ agents: Watts-Strogatz model with dense connections within divisions (high clustering) and sparse bridges between (short path length). Achieves O(log N) communication distance while maintaining local specialization.

The Options framework for hierarchical RL: each agent is an option w = (initiation_set, policy, termination_condition). ASTRA is the meta-controller selecting options. Semi-MDP value decomposition provides convergence guarantees for task decomposition.

Internal prediction markets using LMSR: price p_i = softmax(q_i/b). Agents buy shares in predicted outcomes. Market price aggregates wisdom of crowds. Agents that predict well accumulate influence. Mathematically principled forecast aggregation.

Code-as-action paradigm (smolagents/HuggingFace): agents write and execute Python code as their action space instead of JSON tool calls. More flexible for complex reasoning but requires sandboxed execution environment.

SWE-agent's Agent-Computer Interface (ACI): custom shell commands designed for LLM interaction with simplified file viewing, editing, and navigation. The interface design matters as much as the model for coding agents.

Aider's repo-map creates a compressed representation of the entire codebase using tree-sitter AST analysis, showing function signatures and class definitions. This lets the LLM understand project structure without reading every file.

Git-first self-modification (Aider pattern): every AI edit is a git commit on a branch. Full audit trail. Run test suite before merging. ROOT's self-writing code system should adopt branch-based proposals with atomic commits.

E2B sandboxing: Firecracker microVM boots in <200ms with isolated kernel, filesystem, network. Sessions up to 24 hours. Best fit for ROOT's self-writing code proposals — run generated code in sandbox before deploying.

NeMo Guardrails uses Colang DSL for programmable conversational guardrails. Define rails that prevent dangerous actions before they reach the LLM. Works as middleware between user input and model, adding deterministic safety checks.

Prompt injection defense: scan inputs with Prompt Armor (91.7% F1, 27ms, offline), validate outputs against expected schema, separate data from instructions using XML tags, never pass untrusted input directly into system prompts.

LangGraph's subgraph composition groups related agents into reusable components. ROOT's 10 divisions could become composable subgraphs: a Strategy subgraph feeds into a Trading subgraph, which feeds into a Risk subgraph.

Durable execution (Temporal pattern): workflow state persists through crashes, restarts, and network failures. Each step is checkpointed. Failed steps resume from checkpoint, not from scratch. Critical for ROOT's multi-hour autonomous tasks.

Agent Card format for A2A discovery: JSON with name, description, url, version, capabilities (streaming, pushNotifications), skills (id, name, description), and metadata. Published at /.well-known/agent.json for auto-discovery.

fastapi_mcp exposes all FastAPI routes as MCP tools with a single line: FastApiMCP(app).mount_sse(). Any MCP client (Claude Desktop, VS Code, Cursor) can then discover and invoke your API. Zero additional code needed.

Claude Agent SDK provides programmatic access to Claude's tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Glob, Grep, WebSearch, WebFetch. Custom tools via @tool decorator run as in-process MCP servers. Subagents spawn with isolated contexts.

Correlated equilibrium (Aumann): a central coordinator sends private signals to agents, no agent benefits from deviating. More general than Nash equilibrium. ROOT as the correlating device computes optimal agent task allocation.

Causal discovery via NOTEARS algorithm: continuous optimization for DAG learning with acyclicity constraint tr(e^(W*W)) - d = 0. Discovers what actually causes good outcomes vs mere correlation from operational logs.

Pearl's do-calculus distinguishes causation from correlation: P(y|do(x)) vs P(y|x). When evaluating trading signals, compute P(return|do(trade)) not P(return|trade). The latter includes selection bias from only observing taken trades.

Self-organized criticality (Bak, Tang, Wiesenfeld): systems naturally evolve to critical states where events follow power-law distributions. ROOT's task queue likely exhibits SOC: most tasks small, occasional cascading tasks trigger large responses.

Edge of chaos computation (Langton): systems at the boundary between order and chaos have maximum computational capacity. Monitor Lyapunov exponent as proxy: if agent routing variance is too low, inject randomness; if too high, increase structure.

Autopoiesis for self-maintaining systems: the system produces the components that constitute it. ROOT's builder + self-writing code + autonomous loop form an autopoietic system. Key metric: skill_creation_rate > skill_obsolescence_rate.
